attributes.description: Alerts when `sqlserver.page.life_expectancy < 300` seconds. PLE below 300 is the canonical memory-pressure threshold.
attributes.name: [SQL Server OTel] Page life expectancy low

investigation_guide.blob:
## SQL Server page life expectancy low

### What fired
PLE dropped below 300 seconds during the window.

### Why it matters
Page life expectancy is how long a page stays in the buffer pool. The well-known 300s threshold is a severe memory pressure indicator.

### Triage
1. Correlate with free-list stalls, buffer hit ratio, memory grants pending.

### Remediation
- Add memory.
- Optimise bad queries / indexes.

### Tuning
- 300s; raise for larger memory footprints (some DBAs use 300 * GB_of_buffer_pool / 4).

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-sqlserverreceiver.otel-default
// Instance-level metric: buffer pool page lifetime in seconds
| WHERE metrics.sqlserver.page.life_expectancy IS NOT NULL
| STATS min_ple = MIN(metrics.sqlserver.page.life_expectancy),
       instance_id = MAX(resource.attributes.service.instance.id)
  BY COALESCE(resource.attributes.service.instance.id, host.name, "unknown")
// PLE below 300s indicates severe memory pressure; adjust threshold for your environment
| WHERE min_ple < 300
| KEEP instance_id, min_ple
